host: AMD Radeon RX 7900 XTX (gfx1100/RDNA3) via HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1
change: Output-column-tiled c>1 decode AWQ pack8 GEMV (single + dual, strided + transposed layouts, templated C in {2,4,8}, bf16/fp16). One block per output pack loads each weight pack once into acc[C][8] registers and accumulates all C active columns, so weight bytes/token stop scaling with C. Per-output accumulation order identical to the per-row kernel -> byte-exact. Wired the transposed variants into the c>1 decode hot sites (full-attn QKV/Z force_gemv, shared-expert down/gate+up use_batch_gemv, linear-attn KV/QK), gated tokens in {2,4,8}, env kill-switch HIPENGINE_DISABLE_PACK8_OUTPUT_TILED.

correctness.kernel_bit_exact_gate: tests/test_paro_awq_output_tiled_gemv.py 192/192 PASS (single+dual x strided+transposed x rows {2,4,8} x shapes x threads {64,128} x {bf16,fp16}), byte-identical to gemv_awq_pack8_{strided,transposed} and gemv_awq_dual_pack8_{strided,transposed}
correctness.exercised_probe_c2: gemv_awq_pack8_output_tiled_transposed_fp16 = 1000 calls/step (per-row pack8_transposed 1800->800); gemv_awq_dual_pack8_output_tiled_transposed_fp16 = 400 calls/step (per-row dual 2200->1800; rotate-fused/some linear-attn dual paths use other kernels, not yet covered)

decode_tok_s_aggregate_warmed_median_3ru.note: Per-c-feasible prompt (prompt*c == 512-token fixture); decode 128, warmup 8. ON = output-tiled active, OFF = HIPENGINE_DISABLE_PACK8_OUTPUT_TILED=1 (per-row). Different context per c, so compare ON-vs-OFF within each c, not across c.

conclusion: NULL throughput result (all deltas within +-1.6%, i.e. run-to-run noise). Output-tiling cuts weight BANDWIDTH but not DISPATCH COUNT (per-row and output-tiled are both a single kernel launch per projection), and c>1 decode at these small contexts is DISPATCH-bound (iter484: 9362 dispatches/step at c8), not bandwidth-bound. So the bandwidth win does not move end-to-end decode throughput here. The kernel is correct and a real bandwidth reduction; its payoff requires (a) removing the dispatch bottleneck (C3.0b graph replay + fusion) so the regime becomes bandwidth-bound, and/or (b) longer contexts where per-kernel work dominates per-dispatch overhead. NO retained scoreboard row.

commands.perf_sweep: for c in 2 4 8 (prompt=512/c): [HIPENGINE_DISABLE_PACK8_OUTPUT_TILED=1] HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 python3 scripts/qwen35_batch_retained_bench.py --prompt-length <512/c> --batch-size <c> --decode-tokens 128 --warmup-decode-tokens 8 --json <out>; read measurements.decode_tok_s_aggregate; median of 3 reps

follow_up: Reduce dispatch count (C3.0b graph replay for c>1 device-resident decode step + kernel fusion) -- the actual throughput lever in this regime.
Re-measure output-tiled benefit at longer contexts (agentic focus cases) once a >=2048-token fixture / multi-prompt path is available; full c-sweep later.
Extend output-tiling to the rotate-staged dual and the selected (MoE) transposed kernels for completeness.
